title: a11y close safety: ambiguity + focus-node diagnostics
desc: Close action defaults: multi-match without narrower -> abort with candidate list (like a11y find); after WM_CLOSE if target still alive at 500ms -> dump UIA focused element so the blocker is visible
steps: Ambiguity guard: close detects grap matching >1 windows with no #scope / hwnd: / --nth / --all -> prints candidate list + returns exit 2; caller must narrow explicitly
Never hardcode critical-process list (Code/chrome/etc) as a bandaid -- user rejected this pattern; the narrower-required rule is the proper fix
LogCurrentFocusNode helper dumps UIA focused element (ControlType, Name, AutomationId, hwnd) on post-close liveness check -- surfaces save dialogs, IME composition, focus handoff bugs
Place focus-node logging at both UIA WindowPattern.Close (+500ms) and Win32 WM_CLOSE (+500ms) checkpoints for two-tier visibility
[DEV] Implementation: csharp/src/WKAppBot.CLI/Commands/A11yCommand.WindowActions.cs A11yClose + A11yCommand.cs close dispatch near targets loop

[v6.3+] close/minimize QuickMode: a11y close and minimize now set QuickMode=true on InputReadinessRequest, bypassing FocusStealer probe entirely. Rationale: destroying or hiding a window legitimately changes foreground -- never a false-positive focus steal. Previously generated FOCUS-STEAL false alarms in FocusSentinel at ~10-1134/hr. Fix: A11yCommand.cs InputReadinessRequest QuickMode = action is "close" or "minimize".
author: claude
